subject: Human Resources
title: When to Let Someone Go
description: A micro-lesson that makes When to Let Someone Go usable.

hook.text: A CEO said: 'Every time I've fired someone, I wish I'd done it three months earlier.' Everyone in the room nodded.

buildup.text: Termination should be the last step—after clear expectations, feedback, support, and documented improvement opportunities.

discovery.text: Keeping a poor performer hurts the whole team—top performers lose motivation when they see low standards tolerated.

twist.text: Compassionate termination is possible—treating people with dignity, providing severance, and helping with their transition.

climax.text: The decision becomes clear when you've honestly invested in improvement and gap between expectations and reality hasn't closed.

punchline.text: Sometimes the kindest thing is letting someone find where they'll thrive.

quiz.question: Why is keeping a poor performer harmful to the team?
options: Top performers lose motivation when low standards are tolerated
